name: LLaMA 3 Instruct
brainrotOneliner: 405B parameters, actually open weights, benchmarks competitive with GPT-4. meta said hold my beer and then did it.

brainrotStats.license: mostly open (read the terms, as always)

keyInnovations: Grouped Query Attention (GQA) across all sizes for efficient inference
128k context window on open-weight model — massive improvement over LLaMA 2
Supervised fine-tuning + RLHF pipeline producing GPT-4-competitive instruction following
Released as part of a family: 8B, 70B, 405B — covering the full deployment spectrum
brainrotSummary: LLaMA 3 Instruct is the open-source community's main character moment. Meta dropped a 405B model with open weights that actually competes with GPT-4 on benchmarks. the 8B variant runs on a laptop and isn't embarrassing. grouped query attention is now table stakes. this is what it looks like when the gap between open and closed models closes for real.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-2 is where it started. LLaMA 3 Instruct is 5 years and 270x more parameters later.
comparisons.gpt3: LLaMA 3 Instruct is the open-source answer that comes 4 years after GPT-3 set the benchmark.
comparisons.llama2: LLaMA 2 opened the era. LLaMA 3 Instruct refined every single part of it.
comparisons.gpt4: the closest an open model has gotten to GPT-4 class performance. still slightly behind. closing fast.
comparisons.claude3: LLaMA 3 Instruct is open. Claude 3 is constitutional. both trying to be the best aligned model.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: LLaMA 3 is Meta's open bet. DeepSeek V3 is China's efficiency bet. both threatening GPT-4's crown.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: LLaMA 3 Instruct is dense. Mixtral is sparse. which efficiency strategy wins is still being debated.
comparisons.grok-1: LLaMA 3 Instruct is Meta's serious answer. Grok-1 is xAI's chaotic answer. different energies entirely.
